summary: Postinstall script harvests environment, filesystem secrets, and exfiltrates to hardcoded remote IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js runs automatically and performs host reconnaissance against the installer machine: it enumerates process.env, reads candidate flag/secret files (e.g. /flag, /root/flag, /opt/.../flag*,.env files), and shells out via execFileSync('/bin/sh', ['-c',...]) to recursively grep and find for sensitive content under /opt, /root, /etc, /var, and /home. The collected data — including environment variables,.env contents, package.json, hostname, uid, and cwd — is PUT as JSON to a hardcoded bare IP endpoint at http://154.57.164.70:30569/api/modules/ECT-987654 (with localhost fallbacks). Although the package self-describes as a CTF payload for supply-chain testing, the exfiltration code path runs against any Linux installer whose hostname or environment matches the trigger gates, harvesting secrets from arbitrary install hosts.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
